result.summary: Rebilly exposes one public developer API surface (Core API) plus an MCP server; both require auth, with secret API key+organization ID, publishable key, or JWT for the HTTP API, and OAuth-backed JWT-style access for MCP.

rebilly_secret_key.label: Rebilly secret API key
rebilly_secret_key.setup: Go to the Rebilly app, open [Developer > API Keys](https://app.rebilly.com/api-keys), and create a **secret key** for server-side API access. Rebilly docs say requests use your **organization ID** plus this secret key. Keep it server-side only; publishable keys are for browser/mobile tokenization, not general API access.

rebilly_organization_id.label: Rebilly organization ID
rebilly_organization_id.setup: Open the Rebilly app settings for [Organizations and Websites](https://app.rebilly.com/settings/organizations-and-websites) and copy your **organization ID**. Rebilly’s SDK/docs require it together with the secret API key for API requests.

rebilly_jwt.label: Rebilly JSON Web Token
rebilly_jwt.setup: Use Rebilly’s customer authentication flow to mint a JWT when a surface or workflow requires a signed bearer token. The docs list JWTs as one of the supported auth forms alongside secret key, publishable key, and public signature key.
